channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Senate passes $70B immigration enforcement bill without limits on Trump settlement fund
item.description: The Senate passed legislation to fund President Donald Trump's immigration enforcement agencies early Friday morning, after weeks of delays and fierce backlash to an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund that threatened to derail the bill.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate passed legislation to fund President Donald Trump's immigration enforcement agencies early Friday morning, after weeks of delays and fierce backlash to an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund that threatened to derail the bill.

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The quiz tracked Trump's wins and losses this week. Can you win bigly?
item.description: Plus, Serena Williams, Peabo Bryson, Kalshi and United Airlines make an appearance. Have you been paying attention?
item.content:encoded: Plus, Serena Williams, Peabo Bryson, Kalshi and United Airlines make an appearance. Have you been paying attention?
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: My husband planned our 'just us' trip and it was perfect. Here's why it worked
item.description: For a smooth, rewarding and drama-free couples trip, take as much stress out of the equation as you can — and get comfortable with the fact that a fight may arise (and that's OK).
item.content:encoded: For a smooth, rewarding and drama-free couples trip, take as much stress out of the equation as you can — and get comfortable with the fact that a fight may arise (and that's OK).
item.dc:creator: Amelia Edelman

item.title: Scientists are teaching AI-powered robots to run lab experiments
item.description: Scientists are building autonomous robotic labs powered by artificial intelligence. The goal, they say, is for these robots to take over human researchers' most laborious, time-consuming tasks.
item.content:encoded:
Scientists are building autonomous robotic labs powered by artificial intelligence. The goal, they say, is for these robots to take over human researchers' most laborious, time-consuming tasks.

(Image credit: Jodi Hilton for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: For many fans, the World Cup begins with a pack of stickers. The U.S. is catching up
item.description: Collecting Panini stickers is a World Cup tradition beloved in Europe and Latin America. In the U.S., interest has been building steadily, and this summer, the buzz is bigger than ever.
item.content:encoded:
Collecting Panini stickers is a World Cup tradition beloved in Europe and Latin America. In the U.S., interest has been building steadily, and this summer, the buzz is bigger than ever.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: The Supreme Court has left limited alternatives for protecting minority voting rights
item.description: After a major Supreme Court ruling, state-level voting rights acts and redistricting strategies in Democratic-led states are among the limited ways left for protecting racial-minority voters' power.
item.content:encoded:
After a major Supreme Court ruling, state-level voting rights acts and redistricting strategies in Democratic-led states are among the limited ways left for protecting racial-minority voters' power.

(Image credit: Mike Stewart)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Most K-12 teachers say AI's impact on education will eclipse the internet or computers
item.description: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows many teachers are using AI to save time, but a majority are also worried the technology is making it harder for students to learn to think for themselves.
item.content:encoded: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows many teachers are using AI to save time, but a majority are also worried the technology is making it harder for students to learn to think for themselves.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Senate passes $70 billion immigration enforcement bill, Trump's agenda tests the limits of some lawmakers' support, John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified information.
item.content:encoded: Senate passes $70 billion immigration enforcement bill, Trump's agenda tests the limits of some lawmakers' support, John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified information.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Putin says Russia will bolster air defenses in response to Ukrainian drone attacks
item.description: President Vladimir Putin says Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside his country and cast a cloud over his showcase economic forum in his hometown of St. Petersburg.
item.content:encoded:
President Vladimir Putin says Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside his country and cast a cloud over his showcase economic forum in his hometown of St. Petersburg.

(Image credit: Dmitri Lovetsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: I wrote about George Santos. Then he made a violent threat and lied about it
item.description: NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.
item.content:encoded:
NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/AP)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn
